I’m seeing more software companies, design studios, and consulting firms choose Idaho as a base because the cost-to-talent ratio is better than anywhere else in the West.

Local media fragments further. The Idaho Statesman still matters, but less every year. KTVB has reach but attention is scattered. Your customer isn’t watching one channel or reading one paper. They’re on Facebook, NextDoor, YouTube, podcast apps, and local Reddit threads. You need to be in all of those places with a consistent message, which requires actual strategic thinking, not just buying a TV spot during the 6 o’clock news.

The businesses that win in Idaho over the next 10 years will be the ones who figure out how to operate at coastal sophistication with Idaho economics. That’s the game.

Why does working with a local consultant matter?

Because this market rewards pattern recognition over templates.

I’ve worked with a roofing company in Caldwell, a med spa in Eagle, a CPA firm in downtown Boise, and a food distributor in Pocatello. The tactics overlap (they all need Google Business Profile optimization, they all need a real website, they all need some form of paid acquisition), but the strategy is completely different for each.

The roofing company gets most of its leads from insurance claims and contractor referrals. Marketing’s job is to make sure they show up when a homeowner Googles them after getting a referral from their adjuster.

The med spa is competing against 30 other providers within 10 miles. They need differentiation, patient education content, and a referral program that turns existing clients into advocates.

The CPA firm doesn’t want more clients, they want better clients. We built a strategy around thought leadership and speaking engagements that attracts $500K+ revenue businesses instead of solopreneurs who want cheap tax prep.

The food distributor needed help moving upmarket from mom-and-pop restaurants to institutional buyers. Completely different buyer journey, different content strategy, different sales process.

A local consultant has context. I know these businesses. I know their competitors. I know what their customers care about. I know which channels actually drive revenue in this market and which ones are just expensive ways to feel busy.

I’m not flying in from Portland for a quarterly business review. I’m 10 minutes from your office. We can meet for coffee and iterate on a campaign in real time instead of scheduling a Zoom call three weeks out.

That accessibility compounds. I’ve had clients text me on a Saturday because their website went down before a big promotion. I’ve walked into their retail location to understand foot traffic patterns before building a local awareness campaign. You can’t do that from a national agency hub.

This isn’t about loyalty or supporting local for its own sake. It’s about working with someone who understands your operating environment because they’re in it too.

posts.title: Running a Marketing Team of One (With AI Assist) in 2026
posts.content_raw:
I run Boise Marketing Guy as a team of one. Always have. But in 2026, that “one” includes AI assistance in ways that would have seemed impossible three years ago. I’m not talking about automation in the old sense, scheduled posts and email sequences. I mean real-time collaborative tools that handle the grunt work while I focus on the parts that actually require human judgment. This post is a field report on how I actually work now, what changed, and what didn’t. If you’re a small business owner trying to decide between a solo consultant and an agency, this is the behind-the-scenes view.

What can AI actually do for a solo marketing consultant?

AI handles three categories of work exceptionally well: first drafts, pattern recognition, and research synthesis.

For drafts, I feed Claude or ChatGPT a brief and get back a structured outline or rough copy in seconds. I used this approach for a Meridian HVAC client last month. I needed five blog posts on heat pump efficiency. I gave the AI my outline, key points, and voice samples. It generated drafts in 20 minutes. I spent two hours editing, fact-checking, and adding the specific Idaho context (winter performance at 5 degrees, Treasure Valley utility rebates). Total time: 2.5 hours instead of 8.

For pattern recognition, AI audits are faster than I am. I can feed it a competitor’s last 50 social posts and get back a breakdown of themes, posting frequency, engagement patterns. I use this when a Boise client asks why their competitor is outranking them. The AI spots the patterns, I interpret what matters.

For research synthesis, AI is a reading machine. I can dump 10 industry reports into Claude and ask it to extract trends relevant to Idaho professional services. It surfaces the signal, I provide the local application.

What AI doesn’t do: It doesn’t know which insight matters to the client’s specific situation. It doesn’t feel the room on a Zoom call. It doesn’t recognize when a strategy is technically correct but culturally wrong for the business.

What is AI still bad at in marketing work?

Three things: judgment, relationships, and original strategic thinking.

Judgment is the hard part. AI can generate 10 headline options, but it can’t tell you which one will resonate with a Nampa construction audience versus a Boise tech startup. That requires pattern recognition from years of watching what works in specific verticals and geographies. I’ve tested this repeatedly. AI suggestions are often generic best-practice, not context-specific best-move.

Relationships are entirely human. Clients hire me because they trust my read on their situation. That trust comes from listening well, remembering details about their business, and occasionally telling them their idea won’t work (and why). AI can’t do the Zoom call where a client is frustrated and needs someone to help them think through a pivot. It can’t read the subtext when they say “we need more leads” but really mean “our sales process is broken.”

Original strategy is still human territory. AI is a remix engine. It can combine existing ideas in new ways, but it can’t create a genuinely novel approach based on a deep read of a client’s competitive position and market moment. Last year I advised a Twin Falls manufacturing client to completely ignore digital ads and focus on LinkedIn thought leadership. That call came from understanding their long sales cycle, relationship-based industry, and the specific gap in their competitor set. AI would have recommended a standard B2B funnel because that’s what the training data says.

I’ve also found AI struggles with negative space, knowing what NOT to do. It tends toward more tactics, more content, more channels. Good consulting often means simplifying, not expanding.

How do you structure your work week with AI assistance?

My week breaks into three buckets: client-facing time, production time, and strategic work. AI helps most in production, a little in strategic work, and not at all in client-facing.

Monday and Tuesday mornings: Client calls, check-ins, strategy sessions. No AI involved. I’m listening, advising, course-correcting. I take handwritten notes during these calls because typing on a keyboard changes how I listen.

Tuesday afternoons and Wednesdays: Production time. This is where AI earns its keep. I batch content drafts, ad copy, audit work. I use Claude for long-form drafts, ChatGPT for short-form social copy. I feed it my notes from client calls, give it context, and let it generate first passes. I’m editing, not writing from scratch. This is the time I reclaimed. I used to spend 12 hours a week writing. Now it’s 5-6 hours of editing.

Thursdays: Strategic work. Planning, research, competitive analysis. AI helps with the research gathering, I do the synthesis and recommendation building. I’ll use Perplexity to pull together industry trends, then I write the actual strategic brief myself.

Fridays: Administrative, invoicing, following up on open loops. I also use Friday afternoons to test new AI tools and see what’s worth incorporating. Most aren’t.

The key shift: I can now handle 8-12 active clients instead of 4-6 because the production bottleneck loosened. But I’m not taking on more than 12 because the strategic and relationship work doesn’t compress.

What do clients actually see when working with you?

Clients see the same thing they always saw: a responsive consultant who knows their business and delivers useful work. They don’t see the AI assist, and they don’t need to.

Here’s what a typical client engagement looks like from their perspective: We have a kickoff call where I ask questions about their business, competitors, and goals. I send a strategic brief within a week outlining what I think will work and why. We agree on a plan. I deliver monthly reports, content, or ad management depending on the scope. I’m available on Slack or email for questions. We have a monthly check-in call to review what’s working.

Behind the scenes: The strategic brief is 100% me. The content drafts start with AI and get heavily edited by me. The monthly reports are formatted with AI help (I feed it raw data, it structures the narrative, I rewrite for clarity and add interpretation). The ad copy often starts as AI output that I punch up based on what I know converts for that client.

What matters to clients is quality of thinking and reliability of delivery. AI helps me deliver faster and handle more volume, but it doesn’t change the client experience. I’m still the one who answers the Slack message, takes the call, and makes the judgment calls.

I’m transparent about using AI when it makes sense. If a client asks how I turned around a draft so fast, I’ll say I used AI for the structure and first pass. No one has cared. They care that it’s good and it’s on time.

When does the solo-plus-AI model break down?

This model works for experienced consultants who already know what good looks like. It doesn’t work for beginners trying to scale before they have judgment.

I’ve seen newer marketers try to use AI as a substitute for experience. It doesn’t work. AI will confidently generate a content strategy that’s generic and misses the client’s actual needs. If you don’t have enough reps to recognize that, you’ll ship mediocre work and not know why it’s not landing.

The model also breaks when you try to push past 12-15 clients. At that point, the relationship management and strategic work becomes the bottleneck, and those don’t compress with AI help. You’re either underserving clients or burning out. I’ve tested this boundary. It’s real.

Another failure mode: over-reliance on the assist. I discipline myself to write from scratch at least once a week, usually a strategic brief or a tricky client email. This keeps my thinking sharp. If I let AI do all the drafting, my editing muscle gets lazy and I start missing things.

The model also assumes you’re working with clients who value strategy and judgment over pure execution speed. If your clients just want fast, cheap content, you’re competing with offshore teams and AI-native agencies that can undercut you on price. My clients pay for the thinking, not the typing. That’s the only way this works economically.

Finally, this isn’t a lifestyle business model. I work 45-50 hours a week. AI didn’t give me a four-hour workweek, it gave me a higher ceiling on client capacity at the same hours.

Should a small business hire a solo consultant using AI or go with an agency?

It depends on what you need and how you want to work.

Hire a solo consultant (like me) if: - You want a single point of contact who knows your business deeply - Your marketing needs are strategy-heavy, not just execution volume - You value judgment and context over process and templates - Your budget is $2,000 to $5,000 per month - You’re OK with focused scope (3-5 active projects at a time)

The AI assist means I can deliver more per dollar than I could five years ago, but you’re still working with one person’s bandwidth and perspective.

Go with an agency if: - You need a full-service team (design, dev, media buying, content, SEO all at once) - Your project is large enough to justify account management overhead - You’re spending $8,000+ per month - You need 24/7 coverage or weekend support - You want documented processes and team redundancy

Agencies have overhead I don’t have, but they also have specialist depth I can’t match. If you need a complex web dev project and simultaneous ad campaigns across four platforms, an agency is the right call.

For most Boise-area small businesses I work with, professional services, local retail, B2B companies under 50 employees, the solo model works better. They get senior-level strategy without paying for junior account coordinators and project managers. They get my cell number, not a ticketing system.

The AI assist just means I can serve them faster and handle a broader mix of tactical execution alongside the strategy. It’s not a different service, it’s a more efficient version of the same service.

posts.title: Google Just Killed FAQ Rich Results. Don't Strip Your Schema.
posts.content_raw:
On May 7, Google added a quiet deprecation banner to the top of its FAQ structured data documentation. No blog post, no announcement video, just a banner. Within hours, my LinkedIn feed was split between people declaring schema dead and people declaring FAQ schema more important than ever.

Both takes are wrong, but the second one is closer to right. Here’s what actually changed and what to do about it before August.

What actually happened

FAQ rich results, those expandable question dropdowns that used to puff up your organic listings with three to five extra lines of SERP real estate, are gone. Google stopped firing them in Search on May 7, 2026.

The phase-out runs on a calendar:

  • May 7, 2026: FAQ rich results stop appearing in Search
  • June 2026: FAQ reporting in Search Console and FAQ validation in the Rich Results Test get dropped
  • August 2026: Search Console API support for FAQ rich result data ends

If you have any reporting pipelines pulling FAQ rich result data into Looker Studio or BigQuery, you have about three months before they start returning errors or stale numbers.

What did NOT happen

This is where most of the LinkedIn takes go sideways.

FAQPage structured data itself is not deprecated. Google did not say FAQ schema is dead or broken. There is no penalty for keeping it. The schema.org spec still defines FAQPage as a valid type. Your markup will continue to validate. Your rankings will not move because of FAQ schema, in either direction. Google has confirmed this directly.

What got killed is the display feature, not the data spec. Those are two different things, and the difference matters.

The pattern

If you have been doing SEO for a while, you have seen this movie. FAQ rich results launched in May 2019, got abused immediately by teams stuffing artificial Q&A blocks onto every commercial page, got restricted to government and health sites in August 2023, and finally got killed entirely in May 2026.

HowTo rich results walked the same path a couple of years earlier. Launch, abuse, restriction, deprecation. Google’s response to schema abuse is no longer to police implementation. It is to remove the display feature and move on.

The takeaway for 2026 structured data strategy is simple. If you are adding schema primarily to capture a SERP feature, the feature can disappear. If you are adding schema to help machines understand your content, you are still in good shape.

The AI search angle

The interesting part of this story is not the death of the rich result. It is what FAQPage schema is becoming.

AI systems are extraction layers. ChatGPT Search, Perplexity, Gemini, and Google’s own AI Overviews all need to pull discrete facts out of pages and cite them with confidence. Content that comes pre-labeled as a question and its corresponding answer, in machine-readable JSON-LD, is easier for an extraction system to parse than a wall of prose.

I am not going to quote vendor statistics on AI citation lift, because most of them are unverified. The directional logic is sound, though. If you have real questions with real answers on your page, marking them up as FAQPage continues to help AI systems find them and cite them with the right pairing.

The job description for FAQ schema changed. It used to be a SERP feature trigger. It is now an AI citation aid.

What to do this week

For pages with existing FAQ schema:

Leave it alone. Don’t run a sitewide strip just because Google killed the dropdown. Valid schema costs you nothing. Other search engines and AI crawlers continue to parse it. The maintenance cost of removing it across a large site is real, the benefit is zero.

For your reporting:

Pull a list of pages that historically earned FAQ rich result impressions in Search Console. Note the CTR baseline before May 7. You are going to see CTR compression on those pages over the next few months, and you want to be able to isolate that from any other SEO changes you make in the same window. Six to twelve weeks of data is a reasonable lookback before drawing conclusions.

Audit any Search Console API integrations or BigQuery exports that pull FAQ rich result dimensions. Get those updated before August 2026, or they will start returning errors.

For new content:

Stop adding FAQ sections just to grab a SERP feature, because the feature is gone. Add FAQ sections where you have real questions from real customers that deserve answers on the page. Mark those up with FAQPage schema if you want AI systems to extract them cleanly. The bar is now usefulness, not display real estate.

The bigger shift

Google has been quietly narrowing the set of supported rich results for three years. FAQ is the latest. Product, Article, Recipe, Video, Organization, LocalBusiness, and BreadcrumbList still produce visible enhancements. Most of the rest have been phased out or restricted.

At the same time, AI Overviews now appear on roughly a third of search result pages, and position one organic CTR has dropped sharply year over year. Traditional click volume from high rankings is compressing across the board. The visibility game in 2026 is less about which SERP features you can stack and more about whether your content is structured and useful enough for both Google and the AI layer sitting on top of it.

FAQ rich results going away is not a strategic earthquake. It is a small piece of a larger shift that has been visible to anyone watching the data for two years.

Don’t panic. Don’t strip your schema. Update your dashboards. Keep writing real answers to real questions.

industries.title: Automotive
industries.excerpt: Car owners search differently now. They ask AI assistants about rattling noises, compare shops on Google Maps, and read reviews before calling. We help auto shops show up where decisions get made.
industries.content_raw:
Most automotive shops get calls from the same five sources: Google Maps, word-of-mouth, repeat customers, drive-by traffic, and whoever bid highest on Google Ads that week. The problem is Google Maps rankings shift constantly, word-of-mouth doesn’t scale, and PPC costs keep climbing.

We build systems that put your shop in front of people when they’re searching. That means optimizing your Google Business Profile so you rank for “brake repair near me”, running ads that show up when someone searches for your specific services, and making sure AI tools like ChatGPT and Perplexity mention your shop when someone asks where to get their transmission checked in Boise.

The work is straightforward. We audit what’s working and what’s broken, fix your local listings, build out content that answers real customer questions, and run paid campaigns that actually convert. You stay focused on fixing cars. We handle getting the phone to ring.
industries.content_html:

Landscaping is seasonal, hyperlocal, and driven by immediate need. Someone searches “lawn mowing near me” because they need it done this week, not next month. Your marketing has to capture that intent exactly when it happens.

We focus on local SEO (Google Business Profile, local pack rankings), paid search that only runs when you have capacity, and making sure your site answers the questions people actually ask. Can you handle irrigation repairs? Do you do spring cleanups? What’s your service area? If that’s not clear in 10 seconds, they’re calling someone else.

Most landscapers rely entirely on word-of-mouth and door hangers. That works until it doesn’t. We build a system that fills your calendar during peak season and keeps maintenance clients coming in year-round.

industries.title: Law Firms
industries.excerpt: Most law firms get found when someone searches their name directly. We help you show up when people don't know you exist yet, during the search that matters most, when they need an attorney right now.
industries.content_raw:
Legal marketing is different because trust matters more than almost any other industry. Someone searching for a divorce attorney or personal injury lawyer isn’t comparison shopping like they would for a plumber. They’re scared, stressed, or overwhelmed. Your website and your visibility need to communicate competence and calm before they ever pick up the phone.

We handle SEO so you rank for the practice areas that actually pay your bills. We run Google Ads campaigns that target high-intent queries without burning through your budget on tire-kickers. We optimize your site so the people who land there actually call. And we make sure your firm shows up in AI search results when someone asks ChatGPT or Perplexity who handles estate planning in Boise.

You stay focused on casework. We make sure the phone rings with the right kind of cases.

industries.title: Manufacturing
industries.excerpt: Get found by procurement teams, engineers, and distributors who actually need what you make. We help manufacturers show up in searches that matter, not just vanity metrics.
industries.content_raw:
Manufacturing marketing is different because your buyers are different. They’re not impulse shoppers. They’re procurement managers comparing spec sheets, engineers researching solutions to specific problems, or distributors looking for reliable OEM partners. They search with precision. “ISO 9001 certified CNC machining Idaho” or “custom injection molding low-volume runs.” Your website needs to speak that language.

We build visibility around what you actually manufacture. That means targeting the technical terms, certifications, capabilities, and regional qualifiers that match how your customers search. We also make sure your site answers the questions buyers ask before they pick up the phone: lead times, minimums, certifications, materials, tolerances.

Most of our manufacturing clients start with foundational SEO to own their capability keywords, then layer in targeted ads for higher-intent searches or new market expansion. If you’re dealing with long sales cycles, we focus on staying visible throughout research phases, not just grabbing one click.

industries.title: Medical & Dental
industries.excerpt: Healthcare marketing that gets patients in the door without looking like every other clinic on Google. We focus on local search, reputation, and making sure your practice shows up when people need care now.
industries.content_raw:
Medical and dental practices compete in a brutal local search game. You’re fighting other clinics, hospital systems with marketing budgets, and insurance directories that outrank you for your own name. Most patients search “dentist near me” or “urgent care open now” on their phone while they’re already in pain or need help fast. If you’re not in the top three local results, you’re invisible.

We handle the entire digital presence. Local listings clean and consistent across 60+ directories. Google Business Profile optimized for the searches that actually convert (“emergency dentist”, “accepts new patients”, “open Saturday”). SEO for your service pages so when someone searches “Invisalign Boise” or “family doctor Meridian”, you show up. Reputation management to get reviews flowing and handle the occasional angry patient review without making it worse. Google Ads when you need volume fast or you’re launching a new location.

You’re busy seeing patients. I run the marketing. You get a monthly report, we adjust based on what’s working, and you get more appointment requests without thinking about keywords or ad copy.

industries.title: Moving & Storage
industries.excerpt: People searching for movers want fast answers, clear pricing, and proof you won't damage their stuff. We help moving and storage companies show up when it counts and turn searchers into booked jobs.
industries.content_raw:
Moving and storage marketing is timing-dependent. Someone needs a mover this weekend or storage by month-end. They’re comparing 3-5 companies fast, looking at reviews, checking if you serve their route, and trying to figure out if you’re legit. Your website and search presence need to answer those questions before they call the next company.

We focus on local SEO so you rank for “movers near me” and route-specific searches. Google Ads for high-intent queries when people are ready to book. Reviews and listings management so your reputation shows up clean. And landing pages that explain services, show service areas clearly, and make it easy to get a quote. No tricks. Just making sure the right people find you when they’re actually ready to hire.

Most moving companies we work with need leads now, not six months from now. So we usually start with paid search to generate immediate calls, then layer in SEO and local optimization over time. Storage operators often need longer nurture cycles and benefit from retargeting people who checked prices but didn’t commit yet.

industries.title: Nonprofits

industries.content_raw:
Nonprofit marketing is different. You’re not selling a product. You’re building trust, showing impact, and making it easy for people to take action, whether that’s donating, volunteering, or sharing your story. Most nonprofits are stuck with outdated websites, scattered social media, and zero visibility in search results when someone types “food bank near me” or “how to help homeless youth in Boise.”

We work with 501(c)(3)s, foundations, advocacy groups, and faith-based organizations to get found by the right people at the right time. That means local SEO so you show up in Google Maps, Google Ads that don’t waste grant dollars, and content that actually reflects what you do. We also make sure your donation pages and volunteer forms don’t lose people halfway through.

I run a small team. You work with me directly. We build around what you already have, your website, your CRM, your limited budget. No six-month contracts. No agency upsells. Just clear work that helps you reach more people who care about your mission.

industries.content_raw:
Marketing for pet services is weird because it’s one of the most emotional decisions people make, but they’re still comparing prices and reading reviews like crazy. Someone booking a groomer or finding a new vet isn’t just looking for availability. They want to see your facility, know how you handle anxious dogs, understand your emergency protocols. If your site doesn’t answer those questions in the first 10 seconds, they’re gone.

We build visibility around the exact searches happening in your area. Local pack rankings for “dog boarding near me” or “emergency vet Boise.” Google Ads that show up when someone’s panicking at 9pm about their cat. Content that actually explains your process instead of generic pet care tips. The goal is simple: when someone in your service area needs what you do, you’re the first credible option they see.

Most pet service businesses rely entirely on word-of-mouth and get surprised when growth stalls. We add the digital layer that captures people before they ask their neighbor for a recommendation. You stay focused on the animals. We handle the part where people find you online.

industries.title: Restaurants
industries.excerpt: Marketing for restaurants means showing up when people are hungry and deciding where to eat. We help independent restaurants, cafes, food trucks, and catering businesses get found on Google, social media, and AI search when it actually matters.
industries.content_raw:
Restaurant marketing is different because most decisions happen in the moment. Someone’s downtown at 6pm, pulls out their phone, and searches “dinner near me” or asks their voice assistant for Italian food. You’ve got maybe 30 seconds to show up, look good, and convince them to walk through your door instead of the place next door.

We focus on the channels that actually drive foot traffic and reservations. That means your Google Business Profile is dialed in with current hours, photos that make people hungry, and reviews that respond to both praise and complaints. It means your website loads fast on mobile and has a menu people can actually read without zooming. And it means your ads target people within a 3-mile radius who are searching right now, not next week.

I’ve worked with Boise restaurants since 2006. The work is straightforward: make sure you show up in local searches, make sure what people see looks appealing, and make sure the path from search to table is smooth. No magic tricks, just consistent execution on the basics that matter.

industries.title: Staffing & HR
industries.excerpt: Marketing for staffing agencies, recruiters, and HR consultancies that need to attract both clients and candidates without burning through budget on agencies that don't understand the two-sided marketplace you're running.
industries.content_raw:
Staffing and HR firms live in a weird marketing space. You need employers to trust you with their hardest-to-fill roles, and you need candidates to pick up the phone when you call. Most marketing agencies pick one side and ignore the other, or they dump you into a generic B2B playbook that doesn’t account for how transactional and timing-dependent your business actually is.

We build campaigns that work both sides. That means SEO and local presence so employers find you when they’re desperate for help, paid search that captures both job seeker intent and employer searches, and landing pages that convert cold traffic into real conversations. If you’re doing executive search or niche placement, we focus heavily on LinkedIn visibility and making sure you show up when someone googles your name plus “recruiter” or “headhunter.”

Most of our staffing clients start with Google Ads targeting employer searches (“warehouse staffing Boise,” “IT recruiter Idaho”) and SEO to own the long tail. We layer in job board integration where it makes sense, but we’re not a recruiting platform. We drive the traffic that feeds your pipeline on both sides of the marketplace.

Dwight Davis Consulting LLC works with outfitters, ranchers, lodges, and small-town retail across Central Idaho’s unique landscape.

service_tiers.title: Video & Presentation Creation + Distribution
service_tiers.excerpt: Turn your message into a professional video and presentation, then distribute it across major platforms. Get found on YouTube, Slideshare, and beyond.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

A short marketing video built from royalty-free stock footage, scripted around your business. I handle the production, then convert it into a polished presentation deck and distribute both to high-traffic platforms.

Why this works:

  • Reach beyond your website. Your video lives on YouTube, Slideshare, and other networks where people already spend time searching.
  • Build trust before the phone rings. Prospects see your brand, understand what you do, and form an opinion before they ever contact you.
  • Fast to launch, long shelf life. The video goes live within days and keeps working for months.

What’s included:

  • Professional video creation using licensed stock footage (no amateur iPhone clips)
  • Conversion to a presentation format (PowerPoint-style deck)
  • Distribution to video and presentation-sharing platforms
  • Increases brand visibility across channels you don’t currently own

Who this is for:

Boise-area businesses that want to explain their service visually without the cost of a full production shoot. Works well for service businesses (contractors, consultants, clinics) where explaining the process or outcome is more important than showing a physical location.

You provide the core message and any branding assets. I script, produce, and distribute. You get the files and the published links.

This is a one-time project. Once it’s live, it stays live. No recurring fees, no subscriptions. You own the final video file.

service_tiers.title: Voiceover Video Creation and Distribution
service_tiers.excerpt: Get a professional voiceover video that explains your business, builds trust, and drives calls. We'll handle the script, footage, voiceover, and distribution so you can focus on your work.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

I’ll create a professional voiceover video for your business using high-quality stock footage, a clear script, and a native US voiceover. The video introduces your business, explains what you do, and includes a call to action that drives phone calls or site visits.

Once the video is done, I distribute it to YouTube and convert it into additional formats (like a Slideshare presentation) to maximize your reach. You get a finished video that works across platforms and puts your business in front of people who are searching for what you offer.

What’s Included

  • Professional stock footage: We select high-quality video clips that match your brand and message. Stock footage is an affordable way to add motion and visual interest without shooting custom video.
  • Scriptwriting: I write a script that introduces your business, explains your value, and includes a strong call to action. The script is clear, concise, and built for voice delivery.
  • US-based voiceover: We use a professional male voiceover artist based in the United States. Clear audio, no accent issues, sounds credible.
  • YouTube optimization: The video is formatted and uploaded to YouTube with proper titles, descriptions, and tags so it ranks in search.
  • Multi-platform distribution: We convert the video into a presentation format and submit it to platforms like Slideshare. More places your video appears, more chances people find you.

Why This Matters

Video keeps people engaged longer than text alone. Longer engagement signals to Google that your content is relevant. Video also shows up in search results, YouTube results, and AI-driven answers.

A voiceover video lets potential customers learn about your business, hear your message, and decide to call you before they ever visit your site. It builds trust faster than a wall of text.

YouTube is the second-largest search engine. If you’re not there, you’re invisible to a huge segment of searchers.

How It Helps Your Business

  • Increases discoverability: Your video shows up in Google search, YouTube search, and embedded results across platforms.
  • Builds trust before contact: People hear your message, see your professionalism, and feel comfortable reaching out.
  • Drives additional traffic: Video distributed across multiple platforms creates more entry points to your business.
  • Supports voice search: Clear, spoken content aligns with how people ask questions to Siri, Alexa, and Google Assistant.
  • Boosts engagement: Video holds attention longer than text. Longer dwell time, better rankings.

How I Approach This

I focus on user-first content. The script answers the question: “What does this business do, and why should I call them?” No fluff, no jargon.

The video is built with SEO, voice search, and local relevance in mind. If you’re a Boise plumber, the script and distribution reflect that. The video works as a search asset, not just a marketing piece.

Production is handled by professionals. High-quality footage, clear voiceover, proper formatting. You get a finished product that represents your business well.

Distribution happens across platforms to maximize reach and long-term value. One video, multiple uses.

What Happens Next

After you order, I’ll reach out to gather details about your business, your offer, and your target audience. I’ll draft the script, select the footage, and coordinate the voiceover. Once the video is complete, I’ll send you a preview. After approval, I’ll upload it to YouTube and distribute it across additional platforms.

You’ll get the video file, links to all published locations, and access to your BMG dashboard to track everything.

service_tiers.title: On-Page SEO Optimization
service_tiers.excerpt: One-time optimization of your page's technical SEO, content structure, and metadata. Everything needed to make your page indexable, relevant, and ready to rank.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What On-Page Optimization Does

On-page SEO is the foundation. If your page isn’t technically sound, no amount of backlinks or content marketing will fix it. This is a one-time service that gets your page indexed properly, makes it readable by search engines, and sets up the structure needed for ranking.

We optimize the technical elements Google actually reads: title tags, meta descriptions, headers, image alt text, internal links, schema markup, and URL structure. We also provide content recommendations based on search intent and semantic relevance.

The work is done once. The benefits stick around.

What Gets Optimized

  • Title tags and meta descriptions: written for search intent, click-through rate, and relevance to the target keyword.
  • Headers (H1, H2, H3): structured so the page hierarchy makes sense to crawlers and readers.
  • Image alt tags: descriptive text for accessibility and image search.
  • Internal linking: connecting related pages to build topical authority and help crawlers navigate your site.
  • HTML and URL structure: clean URLs, proper use of canonical tags, 301 redirects where needed.
  • Schema markup: structured data that helps Google show rich results, answer boxes, and AI-generated summaries.
  • Social profile links: integrated for entity recognition and brand signals.
  • Mobile responsiveness: ensuring the page works correctly on phones and tablets.
  • Content recommendations: we’ll tell you what to adjust or add based on what’s ranking for your target keyword.

Why This Matters

Better rankings come from relevance and structure. If your page doesn’t clearly signal what it’s about, Google won’t rank it. If the metadata doesn’t match search intent, users won’t click.

This service fixes both problems. You get a page that’s optimized for indexing, crawling, and user experience. The technical work is handled. You’re left with a page that’s ready to compete.

How We Approach It

We don’t use outdated tactics. No keyword stuffing, no thin content, no tricks. The work follows current Google guidelines and aligns with how AI-driven search systems actually evaluate pages.

We prioritize semantic SEO, meaning we optimize for topics and entities, not just exact-match keywords. That keeps the page stable as search algorithms evolve.

You get a full report of everything we changed. If you have questions about why we made a specific recommendation, I’ll explain it.

service_tiers.title: Infographic Creation and Distribution
service_tiers.excerpt: Turn your data into something people actually want to look at. We create one custom infographic that tells your story visually and drives traffic back to your site.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You’re Getting

One professionally designed infographic. Not a template. Not a generic stock graphic. A custom piece built around your business, your data, and your audience.

We start with research. What story are you trying to tell? What numbers or ideas need to come across? Then we validate the data, structure the narrative, and design something that works on your site, in social feeds, and in third-party placements.

The goal is simple: make your content shareable, linkable, and visible. Infographics do this better than text alone. They get embedded on other sites. They get shared on social. They show up in image search and AI results. They make boring information interesting.

What It Includes

  • One custom infographic designed for your brand and audience
  • Data-driven content researched and validated for accuracy
  • Visual design optimized for readability and engagement
  • SEO structure built in, including local relevance and AI visibility
  • Multiple formats delivered with recommendations for where and how to use them
  • Your branding incorporated so people remember where it came from
  • Distribution guidance so you know how to get it in front of people and earn backlinks

How It Helps

You get more visibility. The infographic works across search, social, and third-party sites. It supports your SEO by attracting backlinks and mentions. It builds authority in your space. And it gives people something worth sharing, which means your brand travels further than it would with text alone.

The Process

  1. Research - We dig into your business, audience, and goals to find the right topic and angle.
  2. Data validation - Every stat and claim gets checked. No filler, no fluff.
  3. Content structure - We map out the narrative flow before any design happens.
  4. Design - Professional, high-quality visual that matches your brand.
  5. Distribution plan - You get clear guidance on where to post it, how to promote it, and how to maximize reach and backlinks.

service_tiers.title: Press Release Creation and Distribution
service_tiers.excerpt: A professionally written press release that gets your news in front of the right people. We write it, optimize it, and distribute it to real news networks and PR sites.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What This Is

Press releases work. They get your business name in front of local media, industry publications, and Google News. A well-written release can drive traffic, build backlinks, and give your site a credibility boost.

I handle the full process. You tell me what’s happening (new service, milestone, event, award, expansion). I write a press release that sounds like news, not a sales pitch. Then we distribute it to PR networks and relevant news sites.

What You Get

  • One premium press release written specifically for your business
  • SEO-optimized copy with natural keyword use and search intent baked in
  • Structured for AI visibility (AEO and GEO signals included so AI tools and local search can find and cite you)
  • Clear formatting with headings, summaries, and key facts that make it easy to skim and share
  • Credibility elements built in (expertise, authority, trustworthiness markers)
  • Strategic positioning to reinforce your brand message
  • Distribution across PR networks and media platforms to maximize reach

How This Helps Your Business

  • Gets you found. Press releases show up in Google News, industry searches, and AI-driven results.
  • Builds authority. Third-party sites mentioning your business = trust signals to Google.
  • Drives referral traffic. People click through from news sites and PR platforms.
  • Supports local SEO. We include location signals that help you rank in local searches.
  • Creates shareable content. Your team, partners, and customers can reference and link to the release.

The Process

  1. Discovery call. You tell me what’s newsworthy (launch, expansion, award, milestone). I ask questions to find the angle.
  2. Research and positioning. I figure out the story, the keywords, and the right outlets.
  3. Writing. I draft a press release that reads like journalism, not marketing fluff.
  4. Optimization. I structure it for search engines, AI tools, and local discovery.
  5. Distribution. I send it to PR networks and news sites to get maximum visibility and backlinks.

Turnaround: 5 to 7 business days from kickoff to distribution.

service_tiers.title: Social Profile Setup
service_tiers.excerpt: Get your business set up on the social platform that matters most to your audience. One profile, done right, with all the branding and access you need to start posting.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You’re Getting

This is a one-time setup for a single social media profile, Facebook, Instagram, X (Twitter), or LinkedIn. You pick the platform, I handle the rest.

Here’s what happens:

  • I create and optimize one profile aligned with your brand (logo, colors, bio, contact info, etc.)
  • You get access to a dashboard where you can manage the account moving forward
  • The profile is ready to use the day it’s delivered

This isn’t a monthly service. You pay once, I build it, you own it.

Who this is for: Businesses that need a clean, professional presence on one platform but don’t want to spend hours figuring out profile settings, image specs, and bio copy.

What platform should you choose? Depends where your customers are. B2B service companies usually want LinkedIn. Local retail or restaurants do well on Facebook or Instagram. If you’re not sure, I’ll recommend one based on your business type.

service_tiers.title: Additional Pages
service_tiers.excerpt: Need more pages optimized beyond your base package? We'll add and optimize extra landing pages at $20 each.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

Sometimes you need more pages optimized than what’s included in your base SEO package. Maybe you’re expanding into a new service line, launching a location page, or need a dedicated landing page for a specific keyword.

This is a simple add-on. We optimize one additional page for you. Same process we use for everything else: keyword research, on-page SEO, content optimization, internal linking, technical cleanup.

When You’d Use This

  • You’ve got a new service to promote
  • You’re opening a second location
  • You want to target a specific long-tail keyword with its own page
  • Your current package covers 5 pages but you’ve got 8 you want optimized

What Optimization Means

We handle:

  • Title tag and meta description (keyword-targeted)
  • Header structure (H1, H2s)
  • Content optimization (we’ll edit what’s there or write new copy if needed)
  • Image alt text
  • Internal linking from other pages on your site
  • URL structure (if it’s a new page)
  • Schema markup where applicable

One page, fully optimized. You can order as many of these as you need.

Delivery

Typically completed within 5-7 business days of order. You’ll see the work logged in your dashboard, and I’ll let you know when it’s live.

service_tiers.title: Additional Gravity Forms
service_tiers.excerpt: Need another form on your site? I'll add and configure a Gravity Form for you, one-time setup, clean integration.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

I’ll add one additional Gravity Form to your WordPress site. This is for when you need a contact form, lead capture form, survey, or any other custom form beyond what you already have.

The form gets configured to match your needs, fields, notifications, confirmations, styling. It integrates with your existing site design and sends submissions where you need them (email, CRM, etc.).

This is a one-time setup. You can manage the form yourself after I install it, or come back for more if you need additional forms down the line.

How It Works

You tell me what the form needs to do. I build it, test it, and hand it over working. Takes a few hours, usually same-day turnaround.

Gravity Forms is one of the most flexible form builders for WordPress. If you need conditional logic, file uploads, calculations, or integrations with third-party tools, this is the setup to use.

Gravity Forms is one of the most flexible form builders for WordPress. If you need conditional logic, file uploads, calculations, or integrations with third-party tools, this is the setup to use.

service_tiers.title: Photo Gallery

service_tiers.content_raw:

A custom photo gallery built into your site. Clean layout, mobile-responsive, fast-loading. Good for portfolios, project showcases, before/after shots, product galleries, whatever you need to show visually.

I set it up once, hand you the controls. You can add or swap photos anytime through your dashboard. No ongoing fees.

Why This Matters

People buy with their eyes. A gallery lets you show your work without cramming everything onto one page. It’s organized, professional, and it loads fast.

This isn’t a slideshow plugin or a third-party embed. It’s built into your site structure, optimized for speed and mobile viewing.

What Happens After Setup

You get access to the gallery manager in your dashboard. Upload new photos, reorder them, write captions. It’s straightforward.

If you need layout changes or custom categories down the road, we can handle that separately. But the core gallery is yours to manage day-to-day.

service_tiers.title: Blog Article
service_tiers.excerpt: One custom blog post, written for your business and optimized for search. You get the content, we handle the research and writing.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

A single blog article, written specifically for your Boise business. I research your topic, write the piece, and optimize it for SEO. You review it, request edits if needed, then publish it on your site.

This is a one-time service. Good for filling a content gap, launching a new topic, or testing blog content before committing to a monthly plan.

How It Works

  1. Topic Brief: You tell me what you want the article to cover. If you don’t have a specific topic, I’ll suggest one based on your business and keyword research.
  2. Research & Writing: I write a 800-1200 word article. It’s optimized for a target keyword, includes internal links if applicable, and reads naturally (not keyword-stuffed).
  3. Delivery: You get the finished article in a Google Doc or Word file. Review it, request one round of edits if needed.
  4. You Publish: You add it to your website. If you need help with that, let me know.

What’s Included

  • 800-1200 words
  • Keyword research and optimization
  • Meta title and description suggestions
  • One revision round
  • Delivered in 7-10 business days

This is real writing, not AI slop. The article is researched and written to actually help your audience, which is what Google rewards.

service_tiers.title: Logo Design
service_tiers.excerpt: A clean, professional logo that represents your business. One flat fee, no monthly billing.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

I design a custom logo for your business. Simple process: you tell me what you’re looking for, I create concepts, we refine until it’s right.

This is a one-time project. You own the final files outright, full resolution, web-ready formats, the works. Use it everywhere: website, business cards, signage, social media.

How It Works

  1. Brief: We talk about your business, your audience, and what kind of look you’re after. Formal or casual. Modern or traditional. Color preferences, anything you definitely don’t want.

  2. Concepts: I create initial design directions. Usually 2-3 concepts so you have real options to choose from.

  3. Revisions: Pick the direction you like, and we refine it. Adjust colors, try different fonts, tweak the layout. We go back and forth until it feels right.

  4. Final Files: Once approved, you get the logo in multiple formats: vector files (AI, EPS) for print and scalability, plus web formats (PNG, JPG) with transparent backgrounds where applicable.

File Deliverables

  • Vector files (scalable to any size without quality loss)
  • High-resolution PNG with transparent background
  • JPG versions for general use
  • Color and black/white variations

You can use these files however you need. No licensing restrictions, no ongoing fees.

Timeline

Typically 1-2 weeks from brief to final files, depending on revision rounds. If you need it faster, let me know upfront and we’ll see what we can do.

service_tiers.title: Call Tracking - Local Number
service_tiers.excerpt: Know exactly what makes the phone ring. You spend time and money advertising your business, but do you know which ads, pages, and keywords are driving inbound phone calls? Call tracking shows you.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

Call tracking gives you a dedicated local number that routes to your business line. Every call that comes through gets logged with full details: which campaign drove it, what keyword they searched, what page they were on, time of day, location, call duration, and a full recording.

You’ll see all of this in your BMG dashboard. No guesswork about what’s working.

What This Tells You

Campaign performance at the call level. See which PPC keywords, landing pages, and ad campaigns are actually generating phone leads. Not just clicks. Calls.

Call quality. Listen to recordings. Find out which campaigns bring longer, more qualified calls that turn into customers. Coach your team based on real conversations.

Timing and geography. See what time of day and which locations produce the most calls and the highest conversion rates. Use that to adjust your PPC schedule and targeting.

Landing page performance. Track which pages generate the most calls and the best leads. Double down on what works, cut what doesn’t.

Budget allocation. When you know which campaigns drive actual phone conversions, you can shift your budget toward the channels that matter.

How It Works

We set up a tracking number in under a minute. It forwards to your existing business line. The number automatically displays to the right audience based on campaign source. You manage everything from your dashboard.

Includes up to 200 minutes per month. Additional minutes billed at $0.09 each.

service_tiers.title: Call Tracking - Toll Free Number
service_tiers.excerpt: Know exactly what makes the phone ring. You spend time and money advertising your business, but do you know what ads, pages, and keywords are driving inbound phone calls? Call tracking shows you.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

When you add call tracking to your account, you get a dedicated toll-free number that routes to your main line. Every call gets logged, tagged, and tied back to the source that triggered it. If someone finds you through a Google Ad, clicks through from your website, or sees an offline ad with the number, we know.

What This Does for You

Track phone conversions from every marketing channel. See which PPC keywords drive calls. See which landing pages convert. See which campaigns are worth the spend.

Record calls for quality and training. Every inbound call can be recorded (where legally permitted). Listen back to qualify leads, coach your staff, or improve how your team handles customer service.

Show the right number to the right audience. Dynamic insertion means the tracking number displays automatically on your site when someone arrives from a tracked source.

Set it up in under a minute. You get the number, we route it, you start seeing data.

Manage everything from your dashboard. Call logs, recordings, source attribution, all in one place.

Why This Matters

You get detailed reports on call volume, trends, and patterns. You find out which marketing strategies generate longer, higher-quality calls that are more likely to convert.

You see what time of day and which locations produce the most calls. This matters when you’re trying to maximize a PPC budget or decide where to run local ads.

You track the performance of every landing page you create. Which pages generate the most calls? Which ones bring in the best leads? Call tracking tells you.

You track conversions from each campaign. Over time, you learn how to spend your marketing budget more wisely because you know what’s working.

service_tiers.title: Business Citations - 25 Submissions
service_tiers.excerpt: Get your business listed in 25 high-quality directories with consistent NAP details. One-time submission that builds long-term local SEO value.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

This is a one-time citation build. I submit your business to 25 relevant directories and citation platforms, making sure your Name, Address, and Phone Number (NAP) are consistent across all of them.

  • 25 directory submissions - Quality over quantity. We focus on directories that matter for local search.
  • Optimized listings - Each submission includes a keyword-aligned business description written for both search engines and actual humans.
  • Category placement - Your business gets listed in the right categories so you show up in relevant searches.
  • Authoritative directories - We prioritize directories with real SEO value, not just bulk submission lists.
  • Full report - You get a detailed spreadsheet showing every directory we submitted to, plus the live URLs where your listings appear.

How It Helps

Citations are one of the core ranking factors for local search. When Google sees your business information consistently across multiple trusted sources, it trusts your listing more. That means better map pack rankings and more visibility.

  • Stronger local SEO - Consistent citations across the web signal legitimacy to Google.
  • Better visibility - Your business shows up in more places: search results, map listings, third-party platforms.
  • Trust signals - Accurate, consistent information builds credibility with both search engines and customers.

How I Approach This

I don’t just blast your business to every directory out there. I pick directories that are:

  • Relevant to your niche - Industry-specific directories where your customers actually look.
  • Location-based - Regional and city-specific platforms that matter for local search.
  • Authoritative - Established directories with real domain authority.

NAP consistency is critical. If your address is listed one way on your website and another way in a directory, Google gets confused. I make sure everything matches across all 25 submissions.

This is about long-term authority, not quick wins. The directories I use stick around and keep passing value to your site.

service_tiers.title: Editorial Guest Blog Catered for AI Search - Up to 400-500 words
service_tiers.excerpt: A single editorial guest blog post, written by specialists and placed on a relevant site. Full content creation, backlink placement, and SEO optimization that works for both human readers and AI search engines.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

This is one guest blog post, done right. I handle everything: research, writing, images, and placement on a relevant, high-quality site.

Each post is 400-500 words of clear, authoritative content written by someone who knows the niche. The writing is structured for readability and optimized for how AI search works today. That means FAQs, entity signals, E-E-A-T elements, and natural keyword integration aligned with what people are actually searching for.

You get proper formatting for SERP performance, a contextual backlink placed naturally in the content, and quick turnaround (5-7 business days).

How It Helps

Guest blogs build authority. They put your name and expertise in front of a new audience, strengthen your backlink profile, and improve visibility in AI Overviews and answer-based results.

This isn’t mass posting. It’s editorial quality. One well-placed piece on a relevant site does more for long-term SEO and credibility than a dozen low-quality links.

The content supports AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) with structured, authoritative writing. It works for GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) with location-aware signals when appropriate. And it drives targeted traffic back to your site.

My Approach

I focus on quality over quantity. Every post aligns with search intent, AI behavior, and actual user value. I follow modern SEO guidelines and avoid low-quality link schemes.

The goal is long-term authority and sustainable SEO growth. One good guest post beats ten mediocre ones.

You tell me the tone (lighthearted, educational, promotional, whatever fits). I take your concept and bring it to life in a way that engages readers and builds trust.

Turnaround: 5-7 business days from order to published post.

service_tiers.title: Keyword Research - Up to 15 Keywords
service_tiers.excerpt: Keywords connect what people search for to what your site offers. Before you chase rankings or traffic, you need to know which 15 keywords actually matter for your business.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What This Is

Keyword research is the starting point. It tells you which search terms bring the right people to your site, and which ones waste your time.

You get up to 15 keywords. These aren’t random. They’re chosen based on what your business does, where you operate, and what your customers actually search for when they need what you sell.

I work with you to understand your industry and customers. You know your business better than anyone. That knowledge is critical. I combine it with competitive analysis and search data to find keywords that actually convert.

What We Do

  • Analyze your current keyword performance to find gaps and opportunities
  • Conduct competitor analysis using trusted tools to uncover high-value keywords
  • Identify high-intent keywords aligned with your services, locations, and goals
  • Focus on search intent (transactional, local, informational) to improve conversions
  • Prioritize keywords based on competition, search volume, and feasibility
  • Map keywords to relevant pages for better structure and on-page SEO
  • Include semantic and entity-based variations for broader coverage
  • Deliver up to 15 targeted, high-impact keywords tailored to your business

How It Helps

  • Improves rankings with intent-driven keyword targeting
  • Supports AI-driven search by identifying conversational and question-based queries
  • Enhances local relevance with location-specific targeting
  • Increases qualified traffic and conversion potential
  • Strengthens overall SEO with a focused, scalable strategy

Our Approach

  • Focus on intent-first strategy over volume-based targeting
  • Align keywords with real user behavior and AI search trends
  • Use data-driven insights with manual validation
  • Prioritize sustainable rankings over keyword stuffing

service_tiers.title: Q&A Creation & Posting (Quora)
service_tiers.excerpt: I write and publish one detailed, research-backed answer on Quora that addresses real questions in your space. It's content marketing that builds trust, earns a contextual backlink, and gets your site in front of people actively looking for solutions.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

One high-quality Quora answer, written specifically for your business and niche. I find a relevant question your audience is asking, research the topic, and post a clear, helpful answer that positions you as the expert.

The answer includes a natural reference or citation back to your site where it makes sense. It follows Quora’s guidelines so the post stays live and the link holds value.

This isn’t just a backlink. It’s visibility on a platform where people go to learn. Your answer shows up in Quora search, Google results, and sometimes in AI-generated summaries. It diversifies your link profile and puts your brand in front of users with real intent.

How It Helps

  • Visibility in answer-based results: Google and AI tools pull from Quora. Your answer can show up in AI Overviews, featured snippets, and search results.
  • Supports Answer Engine Optimization (AEO): Structured, clear answers optimized for how AI systems extract and present information.
  • Generative Engine Optimization (GEO): If your business serves a specific area, I work location context into the answer so it’s relevant for local search and AI responses.
  • Backlink diversification: A contextual link from a high-authority Q&A platform. It’s user-generated signal, which search engines value differently than typical guest posts or directory links.
  • Trust and authority: You’re not pitching. You’re answering. That builds credibility with potential customers who see you helping before they ever visit your site.

The Process

  1. Question research: I identify high-intent questions in your niche. These are queries where your expertise genuinely helps and where your site is a logical next step.
  2. Content creation: I write a structured, engaging answer. It’s optimized for readability and AI extraction. Keywords are worked in naturally, not forced.
  3. Citation and linking: I include a reference or backlink to your site where it supports the answer and follows Quora’s guidelines.
  4. Publishing: I post the answer on Quora with proper formatting. It goes live under an account that looks real and engaged (not spammy).

Turnaround: 5 to 7 business days from order to published answer.

This is a one-time service. If you want ongoing Quora activity, you can order multiple units or we can discuss a recurring plan.

service_tiers.title: Social Bookmarking - 25 Submissions
service_tiers.excerpt: Get your content in front of more people. We submit your site to 25 high-quality bookmarking platforms with optimized titles and descriptions that help people find you.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You’re Getting

This is a one-time submission package. I take one piece of your content (a page, article, service offering) and submit it to 25 relevant social bookmarking sites. Each submission gets a custom title and description written to match what people are actually searching for.

These aren’t random spam directories. They’re real platforms where people browse and discover content. Each submission creates a permanent backlink to your site and gets it indexed faster by search engines.

What Gets Done

  • 25 submissions to quality bookmarking platforms relevant to your business
  • Custom titles and descriptions for each submission, optimized for search intent
  • Proper categorization so your content shows up in the right context
  • Full reporting with live URLs showing exactly where your links are live

How This Helps Your Business

Better Discovery
Your content gets found through channels beyond Google. People browsing these platforms see your stuff.

More Backlinks
Each submission is a permanent, one-way link pointing back to your site. Diversified link profile, cleaner signal to search engines.

Faster Indexing
Search engines crawl these platforms constantly. Getting listed here speeds up how quickly your new content gets indexed.

Brand Mentions
Your business name and site get mentioned across multiple platforms. Builds recognition over time.

How We Do It

No bulk spam. No sketchy directories that’ll hurt your rankings. We focus on platforms that have real traffic and follow search engine guidelines.

Each submission follows the platform’s rules. We write content-driven descriptions that give context, not just keyword stuffing. The goal is long-term authority, not a quick rankings bump that disappears in the next algorithm update.

You’ll get a detailed report when it’s done showing every submission with the live URL where your link is featured.

service_tiers.title: Visual Composer Setup
service_tiers.excerpt: Get Visual Composer installed and configured on your WordPress site. One-time setup, then you're ready to build.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

Visual Composer is a drag-and-drop page builder for WordPress. I install it on your site, configure the basic settings, and make sure it’s working properly with your theme.

This is a one-time setup. Once it’s done, you can use Visual Composer to build and edit pages without touching code.

What This Covers

  • Visual Composer plugin installation
  • License activation
  • Basic configuration (grid settings, roles, post types)
  • Compatibility check with your current theme
  • Test page creation to verify everything works

This isn’t a design service. I’m setting up the tool. What you build with it after that is up to you.

Who This Is For

You’re running WordPress. You want more control over your page layouts without hiring a developer every time you need to make a change. Visual Composer gives you that control.

Good fit if you’re comfortable clicking around WordPress and learning a new interface. Not a good fit if you need someone to build the pages for you (that’s a different service).

Timeline

Setup takes about an hour. I’ll coordinate a time with you, do the install, and walk you through the basics if needed.

service_tiers.title: Website Content Creation & Optimization - Up to 350-400 words
service_tiers.excerpt: Your website needs content that works for both people and search engines. I write 350-400 word pages that inform, persuade, and rank, without sounding like a robot wrote them.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You’re Getting

A professionally written page (350-400 words) optimized for search, readability, and conversion. I research your business, understand your audience, and write content that sounds like you, not like every other site in your industry.

Here’s what goes into every page:

  • Business and audience research: I dig into your niche, your services, and how you position yourself. The content reflects that.
  • Competitive analysis: I look at what’s ranking in your space and match the tone, structure, and intent that works.
  • Strategic CTAs: Every page needs a next step. I write calls-to-action that feel natural and get people to engage.
  • SEO without the jargon: Natural keyword integration, semantic relevance, structured headings. Modern SEO that doesn’t read like a checklist.
  • Answer-focused structure: I write with AI Overviews and featured snippets in mind. If someone asks a question your page answers, I want your content surfacing.
  • E-E-A-T elements: Experience, expertise, authoritativeness, trust. I build these into the content so Google (and your readers) take you seriously.
  • FAQs and internal linking suggestions: I include FAQ schema-ready content and recommend where to link internally for better UX and crawlability.
  • Native English, grammatically clean: No offshore content mills. This is professional-grade writing.

How This Helps Your Business

  • Better rankings: Content written to match search intent ranks better. Period.
  • More visibility in AI search: Google’s AI Overviews and answer boxes prioritize structured, authoritative content. I write for that.
  • Local relevance: If you’re targeting Boise or a specific service area, I optimize for geographic and entity-based search.
  • Higher engagement: People stay on pages that are easy to read and give them what they need. Lower bounce rates, better conversions.
  • Long-term credibility: This isn’t thin content that gets deindexed in six months. I follow Google’s guidelines and write for the long haul.

My Approach

I don’t stuff keywords. I don’t write for bots. I write user-first, intent-driven content that aligns with how people (and AI) actually search.

Every page follows Google’s E-E-A-T standards. Every page is written to support your business goals, not just to fill a content gap.

Quality over volume. I’d rather write one great page than ten mediocre ones.

Turnaround: 5 to 7 business days from the time I get your input.

service_tiers.title: Affiliate Marketing | Basic Plan
service_tiers.excerpt: Start building your affiliate channel with 2 networks, custom banners and text links, shopping cart tracking, and weekly outreach to 30 affiliates. No upfront cost. Cancel anytime.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

This is entry-level affiliate marketing. You get account setup on 2 affiliate networks, custom creatives (2 banners, 3 text links), tracking code installed on your cart, and weekly recruiting. I reach out to 30 affiliates every week on your behalf. You approve or decline them. I send welcome messages, rejection notices, and ongoing newsletters to keep your program active.

You get a branded dashboard where you can watch affiliate signups, track sales, and see which banners are working. Weekly status reports and monthly performance reports land in your dashboard. Email support from my team whenever you need it.

Why Affiliate Marketing

You’re paying for results. Affiliates only get paid when they send you a sale or lead. That’s lower risk than paying for clicks or impressions. You control the commission structure. We handle the recruitment, communication, and reporting.

Who Are Affiliates

Affiliates are bloggers, coupon sites, deal sites, loyalty programs, influencers. They promote your products on their platforms. When someone buys through their link, they get a commission. The network handles all the tracking and payouts automatically.

Networks and Tracking

Affiliate programs run on networks like ShareASale, CJ, LinkConnector, Admitad, Linkshare. The network hosts your banners, tracks clicks with cookies, and pays out commissions based on the rules you set. We place a tracking script on your checkout page so the network can attribute sales back to the right affiliate.

What We Do

We set up your accounts, design your creatives, write your emails, recruit affiliates, manage approvals, and report on performance. You decide commission rates and terms. We execute the campaign.

service_tiers.title: Dynamic Affiliate Marketing Plan
service_tiers.excerpt: Full affiliate campaign management with 6 networks, custom creative assets, weekly publisher recruitment, fraud monitoring, and transparent performance tracking. Built for businesses ready to scale affiliate sales without managing the day-to-day.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You Get

This plan sets up and runs your entire affiliate marketing operation. You get account setup across 6 major affiliate networks, a full suite of promotional assets (banners, text links, mobile creatives), weekly publisher recruitment, fraud monitoring, and detailed performance reporting.

Every week, we reach out to 150 new publishers who fit your vertical. We handle approvals, communicate with active affiliates, send newsletters, and keep your program top-of-mind with partners who can drive sales.

You see everything in your dashboard. Sales activity, top-performing affiliates, banner click-through rates, fraud alerts. The tracking code we install on your checkout page ties every sale back to the affiliate who sent it, so commission attribution is clean.

Why Affiliate Marketing

You only pay commissions when sales happen. No upfront ad spend, no wasted impressions. Affiliates promote your products on their blogs, deal sites, coupon platforms, and social channels. When they send a customer who buys, you pay a percentage of that sale.

The hard part is managing it, recruiting the right affiliates, designing assets they’ll actually use, monitoring for fraud, staying on top of performance across multiple networks. That’s what this plan handles.

What We Build

Creative Assets

  • 5 banner designs (JPG/PNG/GIF formats, multiple sizes)
  • 8 text links tailored to your products
  • 4 mobile-optimized banners
  • 3 dynamic (Flash) banner ads

We refresh these regularly. Affiliates need variety to keep promotions fresh.

Network & Tracking Setup

  • Integration with up to 6 affiliate networks (ShareASale, CJ, Linkshare, Linkconnector, Pepperjam, Admitad, others as needed)
  • Tracking code installation on your shopping cart
  • Google Analytics integration so you can see affiliate traffic in your existing reports
  • Cookie duration configuration (we’ll recommend the right window based on your sales cycle)

Publisher Recruitment & Communication

  • 150 new affiliate invites sent every week
  • Personalized welcome messages for approved affiliates
  • Regular newsletters to keep your program visible
  • Direct outreach to top performers with feedback and optimization ideas

Fraud Monitoring

We watch for suspicious click patterns and fraudulent sales. If something looks off, you get an alert before commissions go out.

Reporting

  • Weekly management reports (what we did, who we contacted, approvals/declines)
  • Monthly performance reports (sales by affiliate, banner performance, network breakdown)
  • Top 5 affiliate stats each month
  • Top banner performance stats

All delivered through your dashboard. You validate sales before commissions are paid, so you never pay out for canceled orders or chargebacks.

service_tiers.title: Affiliate Marketing - Initial Plan
service_tiers.excerpt: Start building your affiliate program with the essentials: network setup, creative assets, tracking implementation, and weekly affiliate recruitment. Launch fast, scale smart.
service_tiers.content_raw:
What You’re Getting

This is the starter package for running an affiliate program. You get set up on one affiliate network, custom banners and text links created, tracking code integrated into your shopping cart, and ongoing affiliate recruitment, 15 new invites sent every week.

I handle the recruitment emails, approve or decline affiliates as they apply, and deliver weekly management reports plus a monthly performance breakdown. You’ll see everything on your BMG dashboard: who’s promoting you, what’s converting, and where sales are coming from.

What Makes This Work

Affiliate marketing is pay-for-performance. You’re only paying commissions when a sale happens. The affiliates, bloggers, coupon sites, influencers, content publishers, promote your product to their audience. When someone clicks through and buys, the affiliate gets a cut. The network tracks it all via cookies, handles payouts, and gives you reporting.

The trick is recruitment and management. Most affiliate programs sit dormant because nobody’s actively inviting high-quality affiliates or keeping them engaged. That’s what this plan handles.

How It Works

Month 1: Full setup. Dashboard account, network integration, banners and text links created, email templates written (welcome messages, approval/decline notifications), tracking code installed on your cart. I start recruiting affiliates relevant to your industry and send 15 invite emails per week.

Month 2 and ongoing: Weekly recruitment continues. I monitor your top 5 affiliates, track which banners are converting, give feedback to your best performers, and deliver weekly management reports plus a monthly sales breakdown.

You get fraud alerts if we spot sketchy activity. Google Analytics gets linked so you can cross-reference affiliate traffic with your other channels.

What You Need to Provide

  • Access to your affiliate network account (or we set up a new one)
  • Product info, logo files, brand assets for creative design
  • Shopping cart access to install tracking code (simple script on the thank-you page)
  • Commission structure and cookie duration (I’ll advise based on your margins and competition)

You validate or cancel sales at the end of each month. The network pays affiliates automatically; you only pay commissions on sales you’ve actually collected money for.

Why This Matters

Banners are the #1 tool affiliates use. If you don’t have them, most affiliates won’t promote you. Recruitment is the other bottleneck, programs don’t grow unless someone’s actively inviting new publishers. This plan covers both: the creative assets and the outreach.

Cancel anytime. No long-term contract.
